aliases: Indian Cottage Cheese Double Onion Curry

english: Indian Cottage Cheese Double Onion Curry

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Paneer Do Pyaza; closest_parent is the general cottage cheese food product class. A paneer curry subclass would be more precise.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP represents paneer as an ingredient; no specific code exists for the prepared Paneer Do Pyaza dish. Closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Paneer Do Pyaza or a sufficiently close parent curry dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Paneer Do Pyaza is approximately 300 g, comprising paneer cubes in a rich onion-tomato gravy, usually accompanied by naan or rice.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Paneer and Paneer-based preparations
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN provides nutrient data for paneer (raw) and for paneer-based cooked preparations. Values for Paneer Do Pyaza are adapted from ICMR-NIN paneer curry reference values, accounting for the double-onion preparation style and typical oil/ghee content. Energy and macros are consistent with ICMR-NIN paneer curry entries (~150–170 kcal/100 g range). Secondary cross-check performed against USDA FoodData Central entry for 'Cheese, cottage, creamed' and generic Indian curry composition data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for paneer and mixed vegetable curry
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Paneer Do Pyaza. GI estimated at 35 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for paneer-based curry dishes and mixed vegetable curries (GI range 30–40, low category). Paneer itself has a very low GI (~27) due to high protein and fat content; the onion-tomato gravy contributes modest carbohydrate with low GI impact. Glycemic load per 300 g serving calculated as (7.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 300 g / 100) × 35 / 100 = 7.77, rounded to 7.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) from salt and spice blends
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g and request reduced-salt preparation, as a 300 g restaurant serving contributes approximately 930 mg sodium, representing roughly 40% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and moderate carbohydrate content (~7.4 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Paneer Do Pyaza has a low glycemic index and a glycemic load of approximately 7.8 per standard serving, making it a suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed with low-GI accompaniments such as roti or cauliflower rice rather than white rice or naan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (~5.4 g/100 g) from paneer and ghee
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of Paneer Do Pyaza, primarily from paneer and ghee, contributes approximately 16 g saturated fat per 300 g restaurant serving; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and prefer preparations using minimal ghee or substitute with unsaturated cooking oil.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Paneer Do Pyaza is a classic North Indian vegetarian curry in which firm cubes of paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) are cooked with a double quantity of onions — hence 'do pyaza', meaning 'two onions' in Hindi — added in two stages: first sautéed at the beginning to form the base of the gravy, and again towards the end to retain texture and sweetness. The dish is finished in a rich tomato-onion gravy spiced with cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, and red chilli, typically cooked in ghee or oil. The result is a creamy, mildly spiced curry with a slightly tangy and sweet onion flavour profile and a soft, yielding texture from the paneer. Nutritionally, Paneer Do Pyaza provides approximately 158 kcal per 100 g, with a meaningful protein contribution of 7.8 g and fat of 11.2 g per 100 g, largely from the paneer. It is a good source of calcium (182 mg/100 g) and provides moderate sodium (~310 mg/100 g). The glycemic index is low (~35), making it appropriate for blood glucose management. Clinically, the dish is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management in controlled portions, but its saturated fat and sodium content warrant attention for cardiovascular disease and hypertension patients. It is naturally gluten-free and vegetarian, and is commonly served with naan, roti, or basmati rice in Indian restaurants across Singapore and globally.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN paneer curry reference data rather than a direct measurement of Paneer Do Pyaza as a named dish. The GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for similar paneer-based curry dishes. No exact match was found in any approved primary source for this specific dish by name.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN paneer curry reference data and is not reliably present in significant quantities in this dish. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this food category. All other -1 values represent nutrients not reported in the primary source. Nutrient values represent a typical home-style or restaurant preparation; actual values may vary depending on the quantity of ghee, oil, and salt used by individual restaurants. The field survey data source (Anjana Kitchen – Six Battery Road) is used only for food identity and context; it is not used as a nutrient source. GI set to 35 (low category, ≤55 threshold) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database paneer curry entries.
